utterance for session {session_id}\")\n\n        # Build system prompt per call from prompts/router.md (+ urgency rules),\n        # so edits to either prompt take effect without a server restart.\n        system_prompt = self._build_system_prompt()\n\n        try:\n            response = await client.call_simple(\n                system_prompt=system_prompt,\n                user_message=user_message,\n                model=ModelClass.SONNET.value,\n                max_tokens=2048,\n                temperature=0.3,  # Lower temperature for consistent classification\n            )\n\n            # Strip markdown code fences if present (ZAI proxy wraps in ```json...```)\n            raw = response.strip()\n            if raw.startswith(\"```\"):\n                raw = raw.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n                raw = raw.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n\n            # Parse JSON response\n            intents_data = json.loads(raw)\n            classifications = []\n\n            for intent_data in intents_data:\n                # Map string to IntentType enum\n                intent_type_str = intent_data.get(\"intent_type\", \"status\")\n                try:\n                    intent_type = IntentType(intent_type_str)\n                except ValueError:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Unknown intent type: {intent_type_str}\")\n                    intent_type = IntentType.STATUS\n\n                classification = IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=intent_type,\n                    project_slug=intent_data.get(\"project_slug\"),\n                    confidence=float(intent_data.get(\"confidence\", 0.8)),\n                    utterance_fragment=intent_data.get(\"utterance_fragment\", utterance),\n                    reasoning=intent_data.get(\"reasoning\", \"\"),\n                    urgency=intent_data.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n                )\n                classifications.append(classification)\n\n            logger.info(f\"Classified {len(classifications)} intents from utterance\")\n            return classifications\n\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Failed to parse router response as JSON: {e}\")\n            # Fallback: return single status intent\n            return [\n                IntentClassification(\n                    intent_type=IntentType.STATUS,\n                    utterance_fragment=utterance,\n                    confidence=0.5,\n                    reasoning=\"Classification failed, defaulting to status\",\n                )\n            ]\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Classification failed: {e}\")\n            raise\n\n    async def route_utterance(\n        self,\n        utterance: str,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n    ) -> list[RoutedIntent]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Route an utterance to appropriate strands.\n\n        Classifies the utterance and routes each intent to the correct strand.\n\n        Args:\n            utterance: The user utterance\n            utterance_id: Unique ID for this utterance\n            session_id: Session ID\n\n        Returns:\n            List of RoutedIntent objects\n        \"\"\"\n        # Classify the utterance\n        classifications = await self.classify_utterance(utterance, session_id)\n\n        routed_intents = []\n        for classification in classifications:\n            # Create intent ID\n            intent_id = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n            routed_intent = RoutedIntent(\n                intent_id=intent_id,\n                classification=classification,\n                session_id=session_id,\n                utterance=classification.utterance_fragment,\n            )\n            routed_intents.append(routed_intent)\n\n        return routed_intents\n\n    async def process_intent(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Process a routed intent by dispatching to the appropriate strand.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with status and data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # For task-profile intents, escalate to bead\n        if classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE:\n            return await self._escalate_to_bead(routed_intent)\n\n        # For other intents, fetch then synthesize\n        return await self._fetch_and_synthesize(routed_intent)\n\n    async def _fetch_and_synthesize(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch context then synthesize into structured result.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to process\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with synthesized data\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Fetching and synthesizing intent {routed_intent.intent_id} \"\n            f\"(type: {classification.intent_type.value})\"\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Step 1: Fetch context — resolve project slug to local repo path\n            fetch_intent_type = self._map_intent_type(classification.intent_type)\n\n            from ..environment.discovery import get_registry\n            repo_path = None\n            ssh_target = None\n            host_alias = None\n            registry = get_registry()\n            if registry and classification.project_slug:\n                entry = registry.lookup(classification.project_slug)\n                if entry:\n                    repo_path = str(entry.path)\n                    ssh_target = entry.ssh_target\n                    host_alias = entry.host\n                    location = entry.display_path\n                    logger.info(f\"Resolved '{classification.project_slug}' → {location}\")\n                else:\n                    logger.info(f\"No repo found for slug '{classification.project_slug}'\")\n\n            fetch_request = FetchRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                context=FetchContext(\n                    project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                    session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n                    repo_path=repo_path,\n                    ssh_target=ssh_target,\n                    host_alias=host_alias,\n                ),\n            )\n\n            fetch_result = await execute_fetch(fetch_request)\n\n            # Step 2: Synthesize result\n            synthesize_request = SynthesizeRequest(\n                intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n                intent_type=fetch_intent_type,\n                utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n                project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n                fetched_context=fetch_result,\n                urgency=classification.urgency,\n            )\n\n            synthesize_result = await synthesize_intent(synthesize_request)\n\n            # Persist result to session store so loadTopics() can display it\n            store = get_store()\n            _topic_type_map = {\n                IntentType.ACTION: \"project\",\n                IntentType.TASK_PROFILE: \"project\",\n            }\n            topic_type = _topic_type_map.get(classification.intent_type, \"research\")\n            topic_id, _ = await store.find_or_create_topic(\n                label=classification.utterance_fragment or routed_intent.utterance[:80],\n                topic_type=topic_type,\n                project_slugs=[classification.project_slug] if classification.project_slug else [],\n                session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            )\n            await urgency=synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n            )\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": synthesize_result.data,\n                \"summary\": synthesize_result.summary,\n                \"urgency\": synthesize_result.urgency.value,\n                \"coverage\": synthesize_result.coverage,\n                \"caveats\": synthesize_result.caveats,\n                \"message\": \"Intent synthesized successfully\",\n            }\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Fetch/synthesize failed for intent {routed_intent.intent_id}: {e}\")\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": routed_intent.intent_id,\n                \"intent_type\": classification.intent_type.value,\n                \"status\": \"error\",\n                \"error\": str(e),\n                \"message\": \"Failed to fetch or synthesize intent\",\n            }\n\n    def _map_intent_type(self, intent_type: IntentType) -> FetchIntentType:\n        \"\"\"Map router IntentType to fetch IntentType.\"\"\"\n        # Map enum values by string\n        type_map = {\n            IntentType.STATUS: FetchIntentType.STATUS,\n            IntentType.ACTION: FetchIntentType.ACTION,\n            IntentType.BRAINSTORM: FetchIntentType.BRAINSTORM,\n            IntentType.LOOKUP: FetchIntentType.LOOKUP,\n            IntentType.REMINDER: FetchIntentType.REMINDER,\n            IntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION: FetchIntentType.SELF_MODIFICATION,\n            IntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG: FetchIntentType.MONITORING_CONFIG,\n        }\n        return type_map.get(intent_type, FetchIntentType.STATUS)\n\n    async def _escalate_to_bead(\n        self,\n        routed_intent: RoutedIntent,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Escalate a task-profile intent to a NEEDLE bead.\n\n        Args:\n            routed_intent: The routed intent to escalate\n\n        Returns:\n            Result dictionary with pending card\n        \"\"\"\n        classification = routed_intent.classification\n\n        # Build escalate request\n        escalate_request = EscalateRequest(\n            intent_id=routed_intent.intent_id,\n            session_id=routed_intent.session_id,\n            utterance=routed_intent.utterance,\n            intent_type=classification.intent_type.value,\n            project_slug=classification.project_slug,\n            context={\n                \"reasoning\": classification.reasoning,\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n            },\n            metadata={\n                \"urgency\": classification.urgency,\n                \"confidence\": classification.confidence,\n            },\n        )\n\n        try:\n            # Escalate to bead\n            result = await escalate_intent(escalate_request)\n\n            return {\n                \"intent_id\": PASSED\")\n    return True\n\n\nasync def test_surface_router():\n    \"\"\"Test surface routing.\"\"\"\n    print(\"\\nTesting Phase 1: Surface Router...\")\n\n    test_db_path = origin_surface_id=canvas_id,\n        urgency=\"normal\"\n    )\n    print(f\"  ✅ Fallback decision: {decision_fallback.reason} -> {len(decision_fallback.target_surfaces)} surfaces\")\n    print(f\" test_map_intent_type_resolves_correctly(self, intent_type):\n        \"\"\"_map_intent_type maps router IntentType → fetch IntentType.\"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n        mapped = test_fetch_command_source_set_matches_matrix(self, intent_type):\n        \"\"\"\n        For each fetchable intent, the source set returned by\n        get_fetch_commands matches the matrix (order-independent). A divergence\n        means an intent fetches the wrong — or no — sources.\n        \"\"\"\n        mapped = IntentRouter()._map_intent_type(intent_type)\n        specs = get_fetch_commands(mapped)\n        actual_sources = {spec.source for spec in specs}\n\n        assert actual_sources == EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES[intent_type], (\n            f\"Source set for {intent_type.value} diverged from expected\"\n        )\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"intent_type\", list(EXPECTED_REQUIRED_SOURCES))\n    def test_required_sources_match_matrix(self, intent_type):\n        \"\"\"Required sources for each intent match the matrix's required=True set.\"\"\"\n        mapped = IntentRouter()._map_intent_type(intent_type)\n        required = set(get_required_sources(mapped))\n\n        assert required == EXPECTED_REQUIRED_SOURCES[intent_type], (\n            f\"Required sources for {intent_type.value} diverged from expected\"\n        )\n\n    def test_expected_sets_are_consistent_with_matrix_definition(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Guard against the EXPECTED_* dicts and FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX drifting\n        apart silently: every fetchable intent present in the matrix must have\n        an expected entry, and vice-versa.\n        \"\"\"\n        matrix_intents = set(FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX.keys())\n        expected_intents = set(EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES.keys())\n\n        # TASK_PROFILE/CLARIFICATION are intentionally absent from EXPECTED_*\n        # (TASK_PROFILE escalates instead of fetching; CLARIFICATION is a\n        # non-dispatched meta-type). Every *other* matrix intent must be covered.\n        non_meta_matrix = matrix_intents  # fetch matrix has no meta-types\n        assert expected_intents == non_meta_matrix, (\n            \"EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES does not cover exactly the fetch matrix\"\n        )\n\n    def test_action_intent_has_required_sources_others_optional(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        ACTION is the canonical 'required source' intent: its required sources\n        are a proper non-empty subset of its full source set, and the\n        orchestrator will treat their failure as blocking.\n        \"\"\"\n        mapped = IntentRouter()._map_intent_type(IntentType.ACTION)\n        full = {s.source for s in get_fetch_commands(mapped)}\n        required = set(get_required_sources(mapped))\n\n        assert required  # non-empty\n        assert required.issubset(full)  # required ⊆ full\n        assert FetchSource.KUBECTL_DEPLOYMENTS in required  # the action-specific source\n\n\n# --- 5. meta-type handling: TASK_PROFILE & CLARIFICATION --------------------\n\nclass TestMetaTypeHandling:\n    \"\"\"\n    TASK_PROFILE and CLARIFICATION are not dispatched to the fetch pipeline:\n    TASK_PROFILE escalates to a NEEDLE bead, CLARIFICATION awaits user input.\n    Neither should resolve fetch commands.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_task_profile_classifies_but_does_not_fetch(self, router):\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"task-profile\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"implement the digest feature\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.9,\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification] = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"implement the digest feature\", \"session-test\"\n        )\n\n        # test_deploy_then_check_yields_action_and_status(self, router):\n        canned = json.dumps([\n            {\n                \"intent_type\": \"action\",\n                \"project_slug\": \"options-pipeline\",\n                \"urgency\": \"high\",\n                \"utterance_fragment\": \"deploy the pipeline\",\n                \"confidence\": 0.9,\n                \"reasoning\": \"first\",\n            },\n            {\n                \"intent_type\": \"status\",\n                \"project_slug\": \"options-pipeline\",\n                \"urgency\": \"normal\",\n                \"utterance_fragment\": \"check if it synced\",\n                \"confidence\": 0.88,\n                \"reasoning\": \"second\",\n            },\n        ])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        classifications = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"deploy the pipeline and check if it synced\", \"session-test\"\n        )\n\n        assert [c.intent_type for c in classifications] == [\n            IntentType.ACTION,\n            IntentType.STATUS,\n        ]\n        assert classifications[0].utterance_fragment == \"deploy the pipeline\"\n        assert classifications[1].utterance_fragment == \"check if it synced\"\n\n\n# --- 4. router → fetch command mapping -------------------------------------\n\nclass TestIntentToFetchCommandMapping:\n    \"\"\"The router maps each intent to the correct fetch source set.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"intent_type\", list(EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES))\n    def test_map_intent_type_resolves_correctly(self, intent_type):\n        \"\"\"_map_intent_type maps router IntentType → fetch IntentType.\"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n        mapped = router._map_intent_type(intent_type)\n        assert mapped == FetchIntentType(intent_type.value)\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"intent_type\", list(EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES))\n    def test_fetch_command_source_set_matches_matrix(self, intent_type):\n        \"\"\"\n        For each fetchable intent, the source set returned by\n        get_fetch_commands matches the matrix (order-independent). A divergence\n        means an intent fetches the wrong — or no — sources.\n        \"\"\"\n        mapped = IntentRouter()._map_intent_type(intent_type)\n        specs = get_fetch_commands(mapped)\n        actual_sources = {spec.source for spec in specs}\n\n        assert actual_sources == EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES[intent_type], (\n            f\"Source set for {intent_type.value} diverged from expected\"\n        )\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"intent_type\", list(EXPECTED_REQUIRED_SOURCES))\n    def test_required_sources_match_matrix(self, intent_type):\n        \"\"\"Required sources for each intent match the matrix's required=True set.\"\"\"\n        mapped = IntentRouter()._map_intent_type(intent_type)\n        required = set(get_required_sources(mapped))\n\n        assert required == EXPECTED_REQUIRED_SOURCES[intent_type], (\n            f\"Required sources for {intent_type.value} diverged from expected\"\n        )\n\n    def test_expected_sets_are_consistent_with_matrix_definition(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Guard against the EXPECTED_* dicts and FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX drifting\n        apart silently: every fetchable intent present in the matrix must have\n        an expected entry, and vice-versa.\n        \"\"\"\n        matrix_intents = set(FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX.keys())\n        expected_intents = set(EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES.keys())\n\n        # TASK_PROFILE/CLARIFICATION are intentionally absent from EXPECTED_*\n        # (TASK_PROFILE escalates instead of fetching; CLARIFICATION is a\n        # non-dispatched meta-type). Every *other* matrix intent must be covered.\n        non_meta_matrix = matrix_intents  # fetch matrix has no meta-types\n        assert expected_intents == non_meta_matrix, (\n            \"EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES does not cover exactly the fetch matrix\"\n        )\n\n    def test_action_intent_has_required_sources_others_optional(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        ACTION is the canonical 'required source' intent: its required sources\n        are a proper non-empty subset of its full source set, and the\n        orchestrator will treat their failure as blocking.\n        \"\"\"\n        mapped = IntentRouter()._map_intent_type(IntentType.ACTION)\n        full = {s.source for s in get_fetch_commands(mapped)}\n        required = set(get_required_sources(mapped))\n\n        assert required  # non-empty\n        assert required.issubset(full)  # required ⊆ full\n        assert FetchSource.KUBECTL_DEPLOYMENTS in required  # the action-specific source\n\n\n# --- 5. meta-type handling: TASK_PROFILE & CLARIFICATION --------------------\n\nclass TestMetaTypeHandling:\n    \"\"\"\n    TASK_PROFILE and CLARIFICATION are not dispatched to the fetch pipeline:\n    TASK_PROFILE escalates to a NEEDLE bead, CLARIFICATION awaits user input.\n    Neither should resolve fetch commands.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_task_profile_classifies_but_does_not_fetch(self, router):\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"task-profile\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"implement the digest feature\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.9,\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification] = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"implement the digest feature\", \"session-test\"\n        )\n\n        # Classified correctly...\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE\n        # ...but its string value is not a key in the fetch matrix, so it would\n        # never resolve real fetch sources (process_intent escalates instead).\n        mapped = r._map_intent_type(IntentType.TASK_PROFILE)\n        # The default fallback is STATUS, proving TASK_PROFILE has no dedicated\n        # fetch mapping of its own.\n        assert mapped == FetchIntentType.STATUS\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_clarification_is_not_a_fetch_intent(self, router):\n        \"\"\"CLARIFICATION is a meta-type with no dedicated fetch mapping.\"\"\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"clarification\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"what?\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.3,\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification] = await r.classify_utterance(\"what?\", \"session-test\")\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.CLARIFICATION\n        # Its value is absent from the fetch matrix.\n        assert classification.intent_type.value not in {\n            t.value for t in FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX\n        }\n\n\n# --- 6. malformed-response fallback ----------------------------------------\n\nclass TestFallbackOnMalformedResponse:\n    \"\"\"A non-JSON LLM response must degrade to a single STATUS intent, not raise.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_unparseable_response_defaults_to_status(self, router):\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, \"this is not json at all\")\n\n        classifications = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"something happened\", \"session-test\"\n        )\n\n        assert len(classifications) == 1\n        assert classifications[0].intent_type == IntentType.STATUS\n        assert classifications[0].confidence < 0.6  # ---------------------------------------------\n\nclass TestIntentClassification:\n    \"\"\"Each pre-canned utterance classifies to its expected IntentType.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\n        \"utterance,canned_response,expected_type\",\n        CANNED_CLASSIFICATIONS,\n        ids=[c[2].value for c in CANNED_CLASSIFICATIONS],\n    )\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def \"confidence\": 0.97,\n            \"reasoning\": \"production health check\",\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification] = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"is weather-fast down?\", \"session-test\"\n        )\n\n        assert classification.project_slug == \"kalshi-weather\"\n        assert classification.urgency == \"critical\"\n        assert classification.confidence == pytest.approx(0.97)\n        assert classification.reasoning == \"production health check\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_unknown_intent_string_falls_back_to_status(self, router):\n        \"\"\"An unrecognized intent_type string degrades to STATUS, not a crash.\"\"\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"this-intent-does-not-exist\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"mystery\",\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification] = await r.classify_utterance(\"mystery\", \"session-test\")\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.STATUS\n\n\n# --- 2. markdown fence stripping -------------------------------------------\n\nclass TestMarkdownFenceStripping:\n    \"\"\"GLM-4.7 wraps JSON in ```json ... ``` fences; the router must strip them.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_fenced_json_parses_correctly(self, router):\n        fenced = \"```json\\n\" + json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"lookup\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"show me the logs\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.9,\n        }]) + \"\\n```\"\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, fenced)\n\n        [classification] = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"show me the logs\", \"session-test\"\n        )\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.LOOKUP\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_unfenced_json_still_parses(self, router):\n        \"\"\"Bare JSON (no fences) must keep working — stripping is conditional.\"\"\"\n        bare = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"reminder\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"remind me\",\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, bare)\n\n        [classification] = await r.classify_utterance(\"remind me\", \"session-test\")\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.REMINDER\n\n\n# --- 3. multi-intent segmentation ------------------------------------------\n\nclass TestMultiIntentSegmentation:\n    \"\"\"A compound utterance segments into multiple distinct intents.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_deploy_then_check_yields_action_and_status(self, router):\n        canned = json.dumps([\n            {\n                \"intent_type\": \"action\",\n                \"project_slug\": assert [c.intent_type for c in classifications] == [\n            IntentType.ACTION,\n            IntentType.STATUS,\n        ]\n        assert classifications[0].utterance_fragment == \"deploy the pipeline\"\n        assert classifications[1].utterance_fragment == \"check if it synced\"\n\n\n# --- 4. router → fetch command mapping -------------------------------------\n\nclass TestIntentToFetchCommandMapping:\n    \"\"\"The router maps each intent to the correct fetch source set.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"intent_type\", list(EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES))\n    def test_map_intent_type_resolves_correctly(self, intent_type):\n        \"\"\"_map_intent_type maps router IntentType → fetch IntentType.\"\"\"\n        router = IntentRouter()\n        mapped = router._map_intent_type(intent_type)\n        assert mapped == FetchIntentType(intent_type.value)\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"intent_type\", list(EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES))\n    def test_fetch_command_source_set_matches_matrix(self, intent_type):\n        \"\"\"\n        For each fetchable intent, the source set returned by\n        get_fetch_commands matches the matrix (order-independent). A divergence\n        means an intent fetches the wrong — or no — sources.\n        \"\"\"\n        mapped = IntentRouter()._map_intent_type(intent_type)\n        specs = get_fetch_commands(mapped)\n        actual_sources = {spec.source for spec in specs}\n\n        assert actual_sources == EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES[intent_type], (\n            f\"Source set for {intent_type.value} diverged from expected\"\n        )\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize(\"intent_type\", list(EXPECTED_REQUIRED_SOURCES))\n    def test_required_sources_match_matrix(self, intent_type):\n        \"\"\"Required sources for each intent match the matrix's required=True set.\"\"\"\n        mapped = IntentRouter()._map_intent_type(intent_type)\n        required = set(get_required_sources(mapped))\n\n        assert required == EXPECTED_REQUIRED_SOURCES[intent_type], (\n            f\"Required sources for {intent_type.value} diverged from expected\"\n        )\n\n    def test_expected_sets_are_consistent_with_matrix_definition(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        Guard against the EXPECTED_* dicts and FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX drifting\n        apart silently: every fetchable intent present in the matrix must have\n        an expected entry, and vice-versa.\n        \"\"\"\n        matrix_intents = set(FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX.keys())\n        expected_intents = set(EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES.keys())\n\n        # TASK_PROFILE/CLARIFICATION are intentionally absent from EXPECTED_*\n        # (TASK_PROFILE escalates instead of fetching; CLARIFICATION is a\n        # non-dispatched meta-type). Every *other* matrix intent must be covered.\n        non_meta_matrix = matrix_intents  # fetch matrix has no meta-types\n        assert expected_intents == non_meta_matrix, (\n            \"EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES does not cover exactly the fetch matrix\"\n        )\n\n    def test_action_intent_has_required_sources_others_optional(self):\n        \"\"\"\n        ACTION is the canonical 'required source' intent: its required sources\n        are a proper non-empty subset of its full source set, and the\n        orchestrator will treat their failure as blocking.\n        \"\"\"\n        mapped = IntentRouter()._map_intent_type(IntentType.ACTION)\n        full = {s.source for s in get_fetch_commands(mapped)}\n        required = set(get_required_sources(mapped))\n\n        assert required  # non-empty\n        assert required.issubset(full)  # required ⊆ full\n        assert FetchSource.KUBECTL_DEPLOYMENTS in required  # the action-specific source\n\n\n# --- 5. meta-type handling: TASK_PROFILE & CLARIFICATION --------------------\n\nclass TestMetaTypeHandling:\n    \"\"\"\n    TASK_PROFILE and CLARIFICATION are not dispatched to the fetch pipeline:\n    TASK_PROFILE escalates to a NEEDLE bead, CLARIFICATION awaits user input.\n    Neither should resolve fetch commands.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_task_profile_classifies_but_does_not_fetch(self, router):\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"task-profile\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"implement the digest feature\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.9,\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification] = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"implement the digest feature\", \"session-test\"\n        )\n\n        # Classified correctly...\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.TASK_PROFILE\n        # ...but its string value is not a key in the fetch matrix, so it would\n        # never resolve real fetch sources (process_intent escalates instead).\n        mapped = r._map_intent_type(IntentType.TASK_PROFILE)\n        # The default fallback is STATUS, proving TASK_PROFILE has no dedicated\n        # fetch mapping of its own.\n        assert mapped == FetchIntentType.STATUS\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_clarification_is_not_a_fetch_intent(self, router):\n        \"\"\"CLARIFICATION is a meta-type with no dedicated fetch mapping.\"\"\"\n        canned = json.dumps([{\n            \"intent_type\": \"clarification\",\n            \"utterance_fragment\": \"what?\",\n            \"confidence\": 0.3,\n        }])\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, canned)\n\n        [classification] = await r.classify_utterance(\"what?\", \"session-test\")\n\n        assert classification.intent_type == IntentType.CLARIFICATION\n        # Its value is absent from the fetch matrix.\n        assert classification.intent_type.value not in {\n            t.value for t in FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX\n        }\n\n\n# --- 6. malformed-response fallback ----------------------------------------\n\nclass TestFallbackOnMalformedResponse:\n    \"\"\"A non-JSON LLM response must degrade to a single STATUS intent, not raise.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.asyncio\n    async def test_unparseable_response_defaults_to_status(self, router):\n        r = _make_router_with_response(router, \"this is not json at all\")\n\n        classifications = await r.classify_utterance(\n            \"something happened\", \"session-test\"\n        )\n\n        assert len(classifications) == 1\n        assert classifications[0].intent_type == IntentType.STATUS\n        assert classifications[0].confidence < 0.6  # flagged as low-confidence\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":431,"oldLines":15,"newStart":431,"newLines":19,"lines":["         Guard against the EXPECTED_* dicts and FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX drifting","         apart silently: every fetchable intent present in the matrix must have","         an expected entry, and vice-versa.","+","+        Compared on .value strings because FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX is keyed by the","+        fetch IntentType while EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES is keyed by the router","+        IntentType — sibling enums that share value strings (the same contract","+        _map_intent_type relies on) but are distinct classes.","         \"\"\"","-        matrix_intents = set(FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX.keys())","-        expected_intents = set(EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES.keys())","+        matrix_values = {t.value for t in FETCH_COMMAND_MATRIX}","+        expected_values = {t.value for t in EXPECTED_FETCH_SOURCES}"," ","         #
